articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Advancing gender and disability inclusive risk knowledge in Burundi
articles.description: To address Burundi's challenge of insufficient disaster data on gender, and disability in relation to vulnerability and exposure, UNDRR gave support to improve SFM reporting, and to enhance the use of inclusive risk knowledge in early warning systems.

articles.title: WFP El Salvador April 2026 Country Brief
articles.description:
Country: El Salvador
Source: World Food Programme

Please refer to the attached file.

KEY HIGHLIGHTS

• Anticipating El Niño impacts, WFP is working closely with government and partners to protect rural livelihoods, as erratic rainfall and the potential for drought threaten crop production, incomes, and food access by mid-year.

• In line with the Government’s priority to advance education outcomes and child nutrition, WFP works closely with the Ministry of Education and key partners to strengthen and further develop the national School Feeding Programme.

• Predictable, flexible contributions help WFP stay ahead of shocks and prevent harmful coping strategies.

articles.title: Oman advances disaster risk reduction and climate action planning through national workshop
articles.description: The Sultanate of Oman gathered governmental and non-governmental representative, and technical experts at a national workshop focused on strengthening disaster risk reduction (DRR) and climate action planning.

articles.title: Sudan National Platform
articles.description: The National Council for Civil Defense has been the focal point for disaster risk management in Sudan since its establishment in 1994. Its membership includes all sovereign ministries, states, and other relevant entities. The Council is chaired by the Minister of the Interior and has a General Secretariat headed by the Director of the Civil Defense Forces.

articles.title: Somalia National Platform
articles.description: On 13 December 2021, the Government of Somalia launched the National Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (NPDRR).The NPDRR in Somalia brings together various stakeholders that are responsible for facilitating disaster risk reduction efforts in the country in alignment with the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction and in coherence with other global frameworks.

articles.title: Euro-Mediterranean partners strengthen cooperation on wildfire risk management and preparedness
articles.description: Representatives from across the Euro-Mediterranean region, alongside Arab Member States convened in Paphos this week for the 3rd Euro-Mediterranean High-Level Workshop on Fire Risks

articles.title: Accelerating local disaster risk reduction in the Caribbean through the work of Making Cities Resilient 2030
articles.description: As global challenges grow in complexity and uncertainty, a coordinated, multilevel approach to international cooperation is essential to support cities and municipalities in addressing systemic risks that cross geographic and sectoral boundaries.

articles.title: Sendai Framework Focal Points and National Platforms
articles.description: By ensuring effective coordination of disaster risk reduction at national level, National Platforms play an instrumental role in ensuring an all-of-government and all-of-society approach to reducing risk and building resilience. These efforts are intimately tied to the coordination efforts at local level (ex. Local platforms), as well as supranational level.

articles.title: Caribbean regional workshop on DELTA Resilience System and the Sendai Framework Monitor
articles.description: To support countries in this endeavor, the United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR), in collaboration with partners, has developed the Disaster & Hazardous Events, Losses and Damages Tracking & Analysis (DELTA Resilience) system.

articles.title: UNDRR 2025 Annual Report
articles.description: In 2025, we marked the 10-year anniversary of the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction and its goal of significantly reducing disaster risk and losses by 2030. This report captures the progress, partnerships, and impact of UNDRR's work throughout the year.

articles.title: Quelimane, Mozambique puts urban resilience into practice with a new DRR action plan
articles.description: The United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (UNDRR), in collaboration with the Municipality of Quelimane, organised an event to integrate technical DRR needs into municipal planning for Quelimane, a major seaport city in Mozambique’.
